title: Demographics (world level)
description: Settlement profiles for a specific world. Gives the narrator concrete numbers (twelve guards, two taverns, one apothecary) instead of vague plurals. Baked content — authored once per world, no runtime generator. PRD §1.2. Lives at sidequest-content/genre_packs/<pack>/worlds/<world>/demographics.yaml.

settlements.description: Every named settlement the players might enter. The narrator references this list when describing the place. Settlements not listed here exist as scenery only — they get a name but no concrete shape.

name.description: Display name. Must be unique within this world.

type.description: Settlement category. Open enum — genre-typed (e.g. 'small_town', 'hamlet' for tea_and_murder; 'arcology_sector' for neon_dystopia; 'station' for space_opera). The narrator uses this to set scope.

population.description: Headcount. Drives plausibility checks ('the town's 47 residents include exactly one blacksmith').

wealth.description: Optional. Open enum — 'destitute', 'modest', 'comfortable', 'wealthy', or genre-specific equivalent. Drives the establishment generator's quality tier when 24-2-era stories wire it in.

profile.description: Optional. Service and institution counts. Authors include the categories that exist; omitted categories mean the settlement has none of that thing.

notes.description: Optional narrator-facing prose (single paragraph). Use sparingly; this is hint material, not lore. Lore lives elsewhere.

profile.description: All fields optional. The narrator references whatever is present. Free-form additionalProperties permitted for genre-specific categories not anticipated here (e.g. 'datacenters', 'fuel_depots').

guard_force.description: Total guards/constables/peacekeepers — the narrator may break out where they are stationed.

shops.description: Map of shop category (open enum: 'blacksmith', 'general', 'apothecary', 'tea_room', 'gunsmith', etc.) to count. Genre-typed.

settlements.name: Glenross
settlements.notes: Train station closed since the 1962 cuts; bus once a week.
